mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes exceeds -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) against rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales and a decrease in the federal funds rate indicates strong consumer demand supported by accommodative policy, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and operational efficiency, which compresses input costs and improves profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, with a cumulative return differential of less than 0.5% compared to contraction periods over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate earnings expectations, which translates into sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days regardless of short-term noise.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 changes show a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 4-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during high-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation combined with stable unemployment forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which raises borrowing costs and suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined inflation-unemployment signal yields positive 20-day returns in over 50% of high-inflation episodes, or the interaction effect vanishes (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payrolls increase below 100,000 signals weakening labor demand that reduces household income growth and consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances where PAYEMS growth drops below 100,000, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below random chance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside expanding industrial production indicate synchronized demand and supply growth that boosts capital expenditure and construction sector earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The HOUST-INDPRO joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of synchronized growth periods, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.
